title: Did Rei II have to die to defeat Armisael
display_title: Did Rei II have to die to defeat Armisael
wikitext:
[]: Did Rei II have to die to defeat Armisael?             Quote Post by Seoul Gamer » Sun May 13, 2012 4:08 am Looking back on that battle, Armisael seemed to be impervious to just about every kind of attack that was launched against it. It resisted gunshots, knife attacks and having that infection ability that would make a melee assault useless against it. Armisael's apparent invincibility is emphasized even more in the manga when Kaworu tries to use a chainsaw against it, and Armisael just infects that too. It just seems to me that there was no way that Angel could have been defeated other than by one of the EVA units absorbing it and then self-destructing as Rei did. Am I correct in concluding this? Are there any other circumstances in which the three pilots could have defeated this Angel without someone sacrificing themselves?

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun May 13, 2012 4:54 am Depends what level of plothax you think Armisael has, and what sort of prep-time and other divergence you allow NERV. Would tag-teaming it into a kill-zone covered by multiple positron beam weapons be enough? Or perhaps just a couple of sufficiently uprated EVA-portable positron beam weapons that would have been able to toast Arael, with whichever of the pilots not getting mind-raped blowing it out of orbit -- then you have the LnY available for the next Angel.

[]:             Quote Post by Seoul Gamer » Sun May 13, 2012 5:10 am Mr. Tines wrote: Depends what level of plothax you think Armisael has, and what sort of prep-time and other divergence you allow NERV. Would tag-teaming it into a kill-zone covered by multiple positron beam weapons be enough? Or perhaps just a couple of sufficiently uprated EVA-portable positron beam weapons that would have been able to toast Arael, with whichever of the pilots not getting mind-raped blowing it out of orbit -- then you have the LnY available for the next Angel.

[]:             Quote Post by svenge » Sun May 13, 2012 6:10 am Armisael's abilities were clearly written so that the desired plot outcome (Kentucky Fried Rei, followed by Rei III's "birth") was the only option available. It boils down to this: Sometimes the meta answer is the only right answer, however unsatisfying it may be.

[]:             Quote Post by Seoul Gamer » Sun May 13, 2012 6:54 am svenge wrote: Armisael's abilities were clearly written so that the desired plot outcome (Kentucky Fried Rei, followed by Rei III's "birth") was the only option available. It boils down to this: Sometimes the meta answer is the only right answer, however unsatisfying it may be.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun May 13, 2012 1:19 pm Non-canon video game example: In the Ayanami with Asuka Raising Project (Asuka scenario is included in the PS2 and Nintendo DS versions of the game), Asuka is able to save Rei II from Armisael if she can pull herself together from the Arael events. Within the context of the series itself, pretty much what svenge said.

[]:             Quote Post by svenge » Sun May 13, 2012 1:40 pm Seoul Gamer wrote: That's more or less what I thought, but some people I've spoken to here seem to regard the battle as something that could have been won under slightly difference circumstances.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun May 13, 2012 1:46 pm For purposes of a fic, NERV or the Angels can have enough firepower to achieve the outcome that the story requires .

[]:             Quote Post by Seoul Gamer » Sun May 13, 2012 2:24 pm Mr. Tines wrote: For purposes of a fic, NERV or the Angels can have enough firepower to achieve the outcome that the story requires .

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Sun May 13, 2012 2:47 pm Here's what I would do: Choke Rei into unconsciousness- "Wait, how does that work?" Well, remember the fight between unit-01 and Bardiel? SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun May 13, 2012 3:20 pm The fact that this was discussed Makoto : The possibility that target will move into the firing range of Unit-0 is 0.02 %.

[]:             Quote Post by User-iel » Mon May 14, 2012 2:28 am Of course, the best practical answer was already contributed. If @ svenge would indulge me extending one other line of reasoning, I would like to submit what I believe to be the most logically consistent answer. N^2. If 'you' are the JSSDF / UN Generals, every Angel needs some thermo(non)nuclear hospitality. In other episodes, it's clear that this option is really not very far off the table. But it has been pushed into a plot hole, and buried. Since NERV / UN forces actually enjoyed much strategic benefit from 'mine' type N^2 weapons, you would think that, budget cuts or no, the outlying Tokyo 3 area would become a veritable termite mound of weapons. Nearly unavoidable, deployed quick-as-you-please, proven weapons. The simple logic being that a first encounter with the bomb might become a rote strategy for increasing Eva's effectiveness. Is Armisael mechanically stronger than Zeruel? I can't say there is sufficient basis to judge. But the in-story Operation Yashima and 'back up plan' to survive Leliel indicates that someone is doing hard math from the presumption that there is such a thing as "enough dakka."

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Mon May 14, 2012 3:00 pm User-iel wrote: Of course, the best practical answer was already contributed. If @ svenge would indulge me extending one other line of reasoning, I would like to submit what I believe to be the most logically consistent answer. N^2.

[]:             Quote Post by Redaye » Mon May 14, 2012 3:31 pm What I enjoyed about the whole scene was that Rei seemed to have made a simple but effective command decision in the field. Simply being in an EVA with an A.T field was no help, as her own body (and Shinji's) developed a degree of invasion. This angel meant business, so time was of the essence. Kamikaze reactionism. Honorable to the last.

[]:             Quote Post by sum_exsanguis » Mon May 14, 2012 3:52 pm IMO Rei II had developed into a closer to normal person. Emotions took over and she acted on them to save the ones she actually learned to care about. That scene also poses the "how does one cry in a liquid question," but that is for another time.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon May 21, 2012 2:42 pm I think the knife attack actually WAS effective: it "broke the skin" and caused significant pain. The problem is that Shinji was emotionally disposed of before he could ... take another stab at it. Of course, it is possible that it would have been futile in the end, but we'll never know.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Tue May 22, 2012 10:34 am I've always been amused that shooting the thing at point blank range did nothing while stabbing it seemed to be effective. I guess ATFs are more like kevlar than bona-fide force fields. I don't see any reason it couldn't be defeated if the pilots were up to snuff. The main issue would be contamination--I have no doubt they could hack it to pieces given sufficient time, but the Angel's mode of attack ensures they likely won't have that time.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue May 22, 2012 10:59 am Bagheera wrote: I've always been amused that shooting the thing at point blank range did nothing while stabbing it seemed to be effective. I guess ATFs are more like kevlar than bona-fide force fields.

[]:             Quote Post by driftking18594 » Tue May 22, 2012 11:29 am Mr. Tines wrote: Now launch a suitably armed EVA into orbit* , and range (and atmospheric attenuation) is less of an issue. Or bring some of those extra generators mentioned in episode 11 on line to power up even more beam artillery, and the balance can shift. *Amusingly enough I thought the idea of EVAs in Space bizarre enough to add this comment to one of the takes I did on the Arael incident: Badassuka 3.0 wrote: “So,” Asuka asked, “are we going to commandeer a Proton from the Russians? Or are we just going to go out and say ‘Come on down if you think you're hard enough.’?”
